instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: God, having spoken of old in many portions and in many ways to the fathers in the prophets,
output: Dios, habiendo hablado en muchas ocasiones y de muchas maneras en tiempos pasados a los padres en los profetas,

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Has at the last of these days spoken to us in the Son, whom He appointed Heir of all things, through whom also He made the universe;
output: al final de estos días nos ha hablado en el Hijo, a quien constituyó Heredero de todo, y por quien asimismo hizo el universo;

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Who, being the effulgence of His glory and the impress of His substance and upholding and bearing all things by the word of His power, having made purification of sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
output: el cual, siendo el resplandor de Su gloria, y la impronta de Su sustancia, y quien sustenta y sostiene todas las cosas con la palabra de Su poder, habiendo efectuado la purificación de los pecados, se sentó a la diestra de la Majestad en las alturas,

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Having become as much better than the angels as to have inherited a more distinguished name than they.
output: hecho tanto superior a los ángeles, cuanto heredó más destacado nombre que ellos.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: For to which of the angels has He ever said, “You are My Son; this day have I begotten You”? And again, “I will be a Father to Him, and He will be a Son to Me”?
output: Porque ¿a cuál de los ángeles dijo Dios jamás: “Mi Hijo eres Tú, Yo te he engendrado hoy”, y otra vez: “Yo seré a Él Padre y Él me será a Mí Hijo”?

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And when He brings again the Firstborn into the inhabited earth, He says, “And let all the angels of God worship Him.”
output: Y cuando trae de nuevo al Primogénito a la tierra habitada, dice: “Adórenle todos los ángeles de Dios”.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And of the angels He says, “Who makes His angels winds and His ministers a flame of fire”;
output: Y de los ángeles dice: “El que hace a Sus ángeles vientos, y a Sus ministros llama de fuego”.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: But of the Son, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever, and the scepter of uprightness is the scepter of Your kingdom.
output: Mas del Hijo dice: “Tu trono, oh Dios, es por los siglos de los siglos; cetro de rectitud es el cetro de Tu reino.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; therefore God, Your God, has anointed You with the oil of exultant joy above Your partners”;
output: Has amado la justicia, y aborrecido la iniquidad, por lo cual te ungió Dios, el Dios Tuyo, con óleo de júbilo más que a Tus socios”.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And, “You in the beginning, Lord, laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the works of Your hands;
output: Y: “Tú, oh Señor, en el principio fundaste la tierra, y los cielos son obra de Tus manos.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: They will perish, but You remain perpetually; and they all will become old like a garment,
output: Ellos perecerán, mas Tú permaneces para siempre; y todos ellos se envejecerán como una vestidura,

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And like a mantle You will roll them up; like a garment they will also be changed; but You are the same, and Your years will not fail.”
output: y como un manto los envolverás, y serán mudados como un vestido; pero Tú eres el mismo, y Tus años no acabarán”.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: But to which of the angels has He ever said, “Sit at My right hand until I set Your enemies as a footstool for Your feet”?
output: Pues, ¿a cuál de los ángeles dijo Dios jamás: “Siéntate a Mi diestra, hasta que ponga a Tus enemigos por estrado de Tus pies?”.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth for service for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?
output: ¿No son todos espíritus ministradores, enviados para servicio a favor de los que han de heredar la salvación?
